stated_goal: Review the first completed provider-backed CodeClash diff offline before any additional paid provider smoke and decide whether future receipts need a stricter diff-quality bar.
acceptance_criteria: No provider, model API, GPU, VM, or CodeClash rerun occurs.
The review reconstructs the p1 modified-file set and key main.py diff hunks from committed artifacts.
The review judges whether the main.py boundary edit satisfies the local task intent.
The review judges whether retaining patch.py should fail future smoke quality gates.
The result publishes a concrete next-bar decision for future provider smokes.
No leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain claim is made.

evidence.notes: Offline summary records zero spend, no model/API/GPU use, reconstructed diff files, task-prompt resolution, and the next quality bar.
Committed iter13 change record is sufficient to reconstruct p1 modified files and key main.py hunks.
Review records that main.py satisfies the narrow Step 1 boundary intent while patch.py should fail future clean-pass quality gates.
Review separates task-quality evidence from game score, leaderboard, SWE-bench, and production claims.
Offline review changed no production or live-domain behavior and created no cloud resources.

falsifiers: The gate must be blocked if committed iter13 artifacts are insufficient to reconstruct the diff.
The gate must be blocked if the review requires another model call or CodeClash rerun.
The gate must fail if it silently excuses patch.py instead of making a future quality-bar decision.
The gate must fail if it uses the game score as model-capability evidence.
The result must not claim leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain verification.
